text: This is a case presentation of transperitoneal laparoscopic partial nephrectomy for a posterior upper pole mass in a sixty seven year old asymptomatic male patient. He presented with a three point five centimeters incidentally detected renal tumor. The CT scan demonstrated right supra posterior pole tumor without evidence of invasion.
The patient is positioned in a modified lateral decubitus position, with visible padding for support. Four laparoscopic ports are inserted into the abdominal cavity for the transperitoneal approach.

The surgeon performs medial dissection of the colon away from the Gerota's fascia. Using blunt dissection, the right gonadal vein and ureter are identified and dissected distally. The dissection continues cranially towards the renal hilum, where the renal vessels are identified and circumferentially isolated.
The surgeon dissects the kidney from its cranial and lateral peritoneal attachments, then mobilizes it posteriorly to facilitate flipping and exposure of the posteriorly located tumor.

The surgeon completes mobilization of the kidney within the Gerota's fascia and flips it postero-inferiorly, exposing the tumor. The tumor borders are clearly delineated, with the renal fat overlying the lesion preserved.

The surgeon scores the periphery of the renal mass with cautery. The renal artery and vein are individually clamped, and the mass is excised from the kidney using sharp cold laparoscopic scissors, maintaining a visible surgical margin of approximately five millimeters around the tumor.

The surgeon performs renorphi using a two-layer sliding clip technique, employing Vicryl 2-0 round sutures and Hem-o-lok clips. The renal vessels are unclamped, indicating the completion of vessel management.
The surgeon secures sutures with Liga clips and closes the Gerota's fascia using interrupted Vicryl 2-0 sutures and Hemolock clips.

The surgeon places the excised mass into a laparoscopic bag and inserts a JP drain within the right gutter. Liver retraction is removed, and the laparoscopic bag containing the mass is extracted through the twelve-millimeter port site.
